decision: React 19.2 + Laravel 12 + MySQL 8 + FastAPI stack
reasoning: Matches spec document requirements and March 2026 stable versions
expected_outcome: Modern full-stack platform with API-first architecture

decision: API-first architecture with versioned /api/v1/ routes
reasoning: Enables future mobile apps and third-party integrations without rework
expected_outcome: Independent frontend/backend deployment and mobile-ready API

decision: Laravel Sanctum for authentication
reasoning: Supports both SPA cookie auth and API token mode in one package
expected_outcome: Seamless auth for SPA frontend and any future mobile/third-party clients

decision: Exercise Group A/B split via hash(user_id) % 2
reasoning: Prevents students from sharing exact answers; ensures 50/50 split deterministically
expected_outcome: Academic integrity maintained without manual assignment overhead

decision: Certificate UUID v4 with public verification endpoint
reasoning: Allows third parties (employers, LinkedIn) to verify without an account
expected_outcome: Trust and credibility for the certificate; blockchain-ready design

decision: Redis queue driver in production, database queue locally
reasoning: XAMPP local environment has no Redis; Redis available in Docker/prod
expected_outcome: Zero-config local dev while maintaining production-grade queue in deployment

decision: Use database queue locally (no Redis on XAMPP)
reasoning: Simplifies local setup — Redis not bundled with XAMPP
expected_outcome: Developers can run queue workers without installing Redis on Windows

decision: Auth API built with Sanctum token auth (register/login/logout/me/forgot-password/reset-password)
reasoning: Full auth lifecycle needed before any protected endpoint can be built
expected_outcome: Secure token-based auth ready for React SPA and future mobile clients

decision: statefulApi() middleware registered in bootstrap/app.php
reasoning: Laravel 12 fluent app builder requires explicit stateful API call instead of kernel middleware
expected_outcome: Sanctum cookie auth works for SPA alongside bearer token auth

decision: CORS configured for machinelearning.local and localhost:3000 with supports_credentials=true
reasoning: SPA at machinelearning.local and local Vite dev server both need CORS access to backend
expected_outcome: React frontend can call API without browser CORS errors in both dev and local production

decision: Admin role guard implemented inline per controller (check role===admin, return 403)
reasoning: No dedicated middleware yet; keeps scope minimal and avoids premature abstraction
expected_outcome: Admin endpoints protected; easy to extract to middleware when role system grows

decision: Settings controllers use updateOrCreate per key — no bulk replace
reasoning: Preserves keys not included in the request; prevents accidental deletion of settings
expected_outcome: Safe partial updates for site_settings and seo_settings tables

decision: All 34 v1 API routes registered: courses, enrollments, sessions, submissions, certificates, posts, admin CRUD, settings
reasoning: Complete API surface built — all public and auth-gated endpoints functional
expected_outcome: Frontend development can begin against stable API contract

decision: Student pages wired to real Laravel API (StudentDashboard, MyCertificates, SessionViewer, QuizPage)
reasoning: Static/placeholder data replaced with TanStack Query hooks using enrollmentsApi.mine, submissionsApi.mine, certificatesApi.mine, axiosInstance session endpoint
expected_outcome: Live data from backend; per-exercise submission form; spinner loading states; correct API response shape consumed via select

decision: Admin pages wired to real Laravel API (AdminDashboard, AdminUsers, AdminCourses, AdminSubmissions, AdminSettings, AdminSEO)
reasoning: All 6 admin pages migrated from placeholder/single-endpoint patterns to correct TanStack Query hooks with select transforms, queryKey arrays, invalidateQueries / refetch after mutations
expected_outcome: Admin panel renders live data; role edit and publish toggle work; grading POSTs to /admin/submissions/:id/grade; settings read/write via /admin/settings/site and /admin/settings/seo

decision: AdminPosts page created — CRUD modal for blog/case-study/news posts
reasoning: Insights/Blog feature required a full admin page to create, edit, delete posts with type badge and publish toggle
expected_outcome: Admin can manage all blog content at /admin/posts; sidebar updated; router wired; postsApi confirmed present in coursesApi.js

decision: Certificate generation via DomPDF queued job (GenerateCertificateJob)
reasoning: DomPDF already installed; queued job keeps grading response fast; idempotency check prevents duplicate certificates
expected_outcome: PDF saved to storage/app/public/certificates/{uuid}.pdf; database record created atomically

decision: CourseCompletionService checks all published group-filtered exercises before certifying
reasoning: Ensures no exercise is skipped; group A/B logic mirrors exercise assignment; enrollment.completed_at stamped on first completion
expected_outcome: Accurate tamper-resistant completion detection before certificate issuance

decision: Certificate download is a public endpoint (no auth required)
reasoning: Certificates are shareable; employers verify without an account
expected_outcome: GET /api/v1/certificates/{uuid}/download returns PDF file download directly

decision: QUEUE_CONNECTION changed to sync in .env
reasoning: Certificate generation must happen immediately on local XAMPP (no queue worker needed)
expected_outcome: Certificates generated inline during grading with no worker process required

decision: AdminAnalyticsController built with 6 KPI stats + 3 chart datasets
reasoning: Provides total enrollments, completion rate, avg score, certificates, students, pending grading, enrollment trend (30d), submissions by status, group A/B distribution
expected_outcome: GET /api/v1/admin/analytics returns structured JSON ready for ApexCharts consumption

decision: AdminAnalytics.jsx fully wired with ApexCharts via window.ApexCharts
reasoning: Greeva vendor bundle exposes ApexCharts globally; useEffect initialises area/donut/bar charts after TanStack Query data loads; each chart instance tracked in useRef for cleanup
expected_outcome: Live analytics dashboard with enrollment trend area chart, submissions donut, group bar chart, and platform summary table

decision: Laravel web.php serves React SPA via fallback route (regex excludes api|storage|admin-css|admin-js)
reasoning: React build output lands in backend/public/app/; Laravel must serve index.html for all non-API paths
expected_outcome: SPA routing works at machinelearning.local/ while /api/ routes remain unaffected

decision: Vite base set to /app/ so built asset URLs are prefixed with /app/assets/
reasoning: React build output is at public/app/; relative asset paths would break when index.html is served from /; /app/ base keeps assets resolvable
expected_outcome: CSS/JS/image assets load correctly when SPA is served at root by Laravel

decision: Public /api/v1/settings/site and /api/v1/settings/seo endpoints added (no auth required)
reasoning: Frontend needs to load SEO meta and GA tracking ID before any auth state is known
expected_outcome: SeoHead component can fetch SEO config on every page load without requiring a logged-in user

decision: SeoHead component added to MainLayout — sets document.title, meta description, OG tags, Twitter cards, and optional Google Analytics script
reasoning: SEO meta must update on every client-side navigation in the SPA; DOMless React component handles this via useEffect on route change
expected_outcome: Search engines and social crawlers see correct per-page meta tags; GA fires on navigation
